old_claim.note: Electrical Lighting Systems provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Existing multiple-arc lighting circuits enabled practical appliance connection through a lamp-socket plug and flexible conductors, although the patent allowed any suitable electricity source.

source_shape.source_locator: Specification and Figure 1: the preferred arrangement removes a lamp, inserts a plug into the lamp socket of a multiple-arc lighting circuit, and connects the iron through flexible conductors; the opening also permits any suitable electricity source.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The patent directly demonstrates use of an installed electric-lighting circuit as the practical connection system for the movable flat iron.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The lighting circuit supplies wiring and a socket interface that make household connection practical, but the patent's broader source language prevents treating that system as universally required.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Preserved and strengthened: electrical_lighting_systems remains an enabling edge, now with primary-source evidence and a bounded non-required claim.
would_reject_if: The node is later narrowed to an appliance powered only by a dedicated non-lighting circuit.
The patent figure is shown not to depict a lamp-socket connection to a multiple-arc lighting system.
The edge is promoted to required despite the patent allowing any suitable source of electricity.
short_reason: The patent directly documents the lighting-circuit connection.
